title: When Adam was sleeping in the South Pole
display_title: When Adam was sleeping in the South Pole
wikitext:
[]: When Adam was sleeping in the South Pole....             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Aug 25, 2009 5:32 pm How did they know where to find him, and how did they manage to wake his grumpy ass up?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Aug 25, 2009 5:33 pm DSS. And as generic or cheap as it sounds, Im going to say part of it is SEELE knows everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Tue Aug 25, 2009 5:53 pm I kinda got the impression they just sort of tripped over him while digging in the Arctic for something else, then sent a team in to investigate

[]:             Quote Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Tue Aug 25, 2009 6:33 pm Don't forget Japan is one of the world leaders of exploration down there.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Aug 25, 2009 9:07 pm I thought his location was mentioned in the Dead Sea scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Aug 25, 2009 9:40 pm child of Lilith wrote: I thought her location was mentioned in the Secret Dead Sea scrolls.

[]: Re: When Adam was sleeping in the South Pole....             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Aug 26, 2009 8:12 am CyberXIII wrote: How did they know where to find him, and how did they manage to wake his grumpy ass up?

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Wed Aug 26, 2009 6:35 pm The whole 'everything predicted by the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls' thing never sat well with me. According to CI, the scrolls originated from the FAR, or at least the information contained within (possble from one of the moons, who knows), but basically, if the FAR could predict with such accuracy the events that transpred in Eva, then as a prerequisite then would have known that Lillith would crash into Adam's new home, something CI makes very clear the FAR didn't want to happen...so why didn't they stop it? I always figured the SDSS where more a sort of User Manual that explained about the FAR, the moons, and the seeds, and SEELE used the information ("keep Life and Knowledge away form each other at all times, or you will be tanged" etc) to work out a scenario leading to instrumentality But I digress

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Aug 26, 2009 8:01 pm I've always assumed that the SDSS predicted what forms the Angels would take, and what they might do to the Lilin. This allows for most of the predictions to come true, and let's Gendo point out that they don't predict everything: They're just about defeating the Angels to stave off 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Thu Aug 27, 2009 10:50 am I thought the Dead Sea Scrolls were like... emergency manuals in case something bad happened. In that sense, they probably have descriptions of multiple possible scenarios, and Seele just put two and two together and figured out "Oh, we're scenario #368". ^^; Also, since Adam was supposed to land there, clues to her location were probably left by the FAR. As for Lilith, we could probably assume the Japanese stumbled across her, because her sphere-shaped ride is quite big, and I think if she'd landed in any other country, they'd have found her eventually as well. I mean, geologists would figure it out, no? For all we know, all of the seeds get sent to the poles of their target planets, and Lilith (being an accident, unless you believe my fanfic) ended up randomly in Japan. Or rather, she ended up somewhere that shifted over millions of years to become what we now know as Japan. My thoughts. I could be full of shit.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Aug 27, 2009 1:39 pm I thought the Dead Sea Scrolls were like... emergency manuals in case something bad happened. In that sense, they probably have descriptions of multiple possible scenarios, and Seele just put two and two together and figured out "Oh, we're scenario #368". ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Aug 27, 2009 2:03 pm ^ Beat me to it... Chapter 12 If you find a suspended Seed of Life, take care to not remove the Modulating Spear (see Appendix D, Modulating Spear, Use Of)... I figured, though, that the SDSS must have been copied at some point by an errant esthete who couldn't help inserting some of his own mythology into the mix. Unless Longinus was the guy that swiped Lilith's LnY and used it to end Christ's suffering, or SEELE just decided to assign Christian themes for the lulz.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 27, 2009 2:21 pm I just wonder what kind of language they were written in...

[]: Re: When Adam was sleeping in the South Pole....             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 28, 2009 7:35 am CyberXIII wrote: How did they know where to find him

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 28, 2009 1:29 pm I figured, though, that the SDSS must have been copied at some point by an errant esthete who couldn't help inserting some of his own mythology into the mix. Unless Longinus was the guy that swiped Lilith's LnY and used it to end Christ's suffering, or SEELE just decided to assign Christian themes for the lulz.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 28, 2009 5:05 pm I always thought that final image in the OP was the SDSS in some Angelic script and maybe the scrolls were later on translated somehow into Latin or something. /My 2 cents.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 28, 2009 11:15 pm Just so everybody knows... Yeah, the final cut of the OP is the SDSS. This is ancient FGC crap, everybody should know it! http://www.evacommentary.org/op/op_2.html#cut085 Topic tiem. Bump an SDSS thread if you wanna talk about that.

[]: Re: When Adam was sleeping in the South Pole....             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Aug 29, 2009 1:17 pm CyberXIII wrote: How did they know where to find him, and how did they manage to wake his grumpy ass up?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Aug 31, 2009 6:13 am ath wrote: I just wonder what kind of language they were written in...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 31, 2009 7:45 am Allemann wrote: Reichu wrote: Just so everybody knows... Yeah, the final cut of the OP is the SDSS. This is ancient FGC crap, everybody should know it! http://www.evacommentary.org/op/op_2.html#cut085 Topic tiem. Bump an SDSS thread if you wanna talk about that.
